title: service-accounts — trait composition
description: One owned trait. `IsServiceAccount` is a marker composed by the ServiceAccount model itself. Ships no accessor / mutator / scope of its own — those live on the model directly — but participates in reflection-based tooling (audit redactors, seeders, factories) that need to enumerate every SA-shaped row without hard-coding the class. NO BelongsToTenant (service accounts OPTIONALLY carry tenant_id but the global scope is deliberately absent — platform-admin surfaces need to see cross-tenant SAs). NO HasCredentialLifecycle (the Identity substrate's lifecycle is human-centric; SAs have their own SecretRotator-driven flow instead).

owned.description: Marker trait composed by the ServiceAccount model. Provides the shared shape identifier for reflection-based tooling. Adds no accessor / mutator / scope of its own — accessors live directly on the model. Used by ServiceAccountFingerprinter to identify SA-shaped principals at the audit middleware layer.

framework.reason: SAs retain a retention hold (default 90 days after deleted_at) before hard delete so downstream compliance queries can still resolve historical audit trails.

SA is never a mail-notification recipient itself, but Notifiable is composed so downstream modules can route notifications ABOUT the SA (dispatched to Application owners) via the standard framework primitives.

notes: NO BelongsToTenant — while `tenant_id` is populated on tenant-scoped SAs, the trait's global scope is deliberately absent because platform-admin surfaces need to enumerate cross-tenant SAs. Tenant-scope filtering, when needed, is explicit at the query builder.

NO HasCredentialLifecycle — the trait's methods (verifyPassword, lock, enrollMfa, ...) are human-centric. SAs have their own SecretRotator-driven flow that lives on `SecretRotator` service, not on the model.
NO IsPlatformUser — an SA is a distinct principal class from PlatformUser. They both authenticate to the platform_admin guard, but they're separate models with separate lifecycles.

The order matters: `IsServiceAccount` runs BEFORE `HasCredentialLifecycle`-like helpers so the marker trait's static hooks fire before any downstream trait's booted() wiring. Because we don't compose HasCredentialLifecycle, this is a defensive ordering rule for future-added traits.

`HasActivityLog` + `HasAudit` compose so SA mutations flow through the compliance timeline. PII fields are trivial to redact here because there are no PII fields — SAs have no name of a human, no email, no phone.
